mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending economic contraction, causing investors to price in lower corporate earnings and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in > 65% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.5pp over 3 months, invalidating the recession-fear transmission channel.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of rising retail sales and falling interest rates amplifies consumer spending power and corporate profit margins, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to generate a positive direction accuracy above 55% in out-of-sample testing, or the interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding industrial production reflects strengthening manufacturing output and supply-side resilience, which triggers short-term positive momentum in equity markets over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to predict positive 5-day returns during recovery phases, with a hit rate below 50% across all post-recession periods since 2000.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real GDP growth indicates robust aggregate demand and corporate revenue expansion, which translates into positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDPC1 growth shows zero or negative correlation with 60-day forward equity returns over a 5-year rolling window, breaking the earnings-growth linkage.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation triggers expectations of tighter monetary policy and higher discount rates, forcing a repricing of equities and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Equity markets consistently rally following CPIAUCSL MoM spikes above 0.4%, yielding positive 20-day returns in > 70% of cases and negating the rate-hike fear mechanism.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in total nonfarm payrolls and industrial production confirms broad-based economic strength, supporting higher corporate earnings forecasts and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined PAYEMS and INDPRO growth signal yields a negative or flat 60-day return distribution, with a Sharpe ratio below 0.2 in backtests, indicating decoupling from equity valuations.

mechanism_narrative: Declining housing starts signal weakening consumer confidence and reduced construction sector activity, acting as a leading indicator of economic deceleration that produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: HOUST declines are followed by positive 5-day equity returns in > 60% of observations during late-cycle phases, invalidating the leading indicator premise for short-term equity pricing.
